work_items.summary: Feature deduplication audit — root-cause investigation + structural safeguard + regression tests
work_items.root_cause:
A high-severity risk was raised: IC-Weighted Dynamic Signal Scoring appeared twice in the active feature list under slightly different formulations (C P2), and ~10 other features (Connection Manager, AccountDecisionAgent, Per-Account Config Profiles, WebSocket Signal Broadcaster, etc.) appeared duplicated across active and backlog lists.

Investigation: Checked backlog.json (8 items, clean), Decifer_Feature_Plan.xlsx (25 items across Phases 1–5, none of the flagged features present), docs/PRODUCT_DEFINITION.md (clean Phase B/C/D roadmap), roadmap/ (8 markdown files, no duplicates), and full git history. Finding: the duplicated features existed in a previous planning era (pre-a7528b8), specifically the multi-account Phase 4 planning documents. The backlog.json introduced in commit a7528b8 was a clean rebuild from scratch — none of those duplicate entries were carried forward. The current state is already clean.

Gap identified: validate_wip() had no structural guard against duplicate IDs or duplicate titles being re-introduced into backlog.json. This is the live regression risk.
work_items.resolution: Added DUPLICATE_ID and DUPLICATE_TITLE checks inside validate_wip() in wip_tracker.py. Duplicate ID detection counts occurrences of each item ID and fires if any appear more than once. Duplicate title detection normalises case and whitespace before comparison, so reformulations like 'IC-Weighted Signal Scoring' vs 'ic-weighted signal scoring' are caught. Added 11 new unit tests covering: exact duplicate ID, count in violation message, unique IDs clean, exact duplicate title, case-insensitive title match, whitespace-normalised title match, violation includes both IDs, unique titles clean, both violation types coexisting. Added 2 real-backlog compliance tests: test_real_backlog_has_no_duplicate_ids and test_real_backlog_has_no_duplicate_titles. These run against the live backlog.json on every test run.

notes: Pending Amit approval before commit. The duplicate features flagged in the risk report are not present in the current codebase — the risk was from a previous planning era that was superseded by the clean 8-item roadmap in backlog.json. The structural safeguard added today ensures that if duplicates are accidentally reintroduced, validate_wip() will catch them and the real-backlog compliance tests will fail fast.
